experiment.createFailed: Could not save experiment. Please try again.
hero.subtitle: Run one prompt across multiple builders—live output where your integrations support it, benchmark-style previews elsewhere—then compare results side by side.
help.orchestrationSignedIn: Signed in: your runs use full orchestration (database experiment ID). The Compare page is a feature matrix only — it does not start builders.
help.comparePageNote: The /compare page compares product features; live multi-builder runs start from the home prompt above.
hero.promptPlaceholder: Describe your app idea… e.g. 'Build a project management tool with Kanban boards, team chat, and Stripe billing'
hero.trustReal: Live v0 runs plus consistent benchmark previews
hero.trustReferrals: We earn via referrals — you keep control
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Straight answers on pricing, builders, scoring, and how we stay unbiased.
faq.a1: Yes! You can run up to 3 comparison experiments for free without an account. Sign up to unlock unlimited experiments.
faq.q2: Which AI app builders do you support?
faq.a2: v0 runs are live today. Other builders are shown as benchmark profiles using the same prompt and scoring model while direct integrations are being rolled out.
faq.a3: Each build is evaluated by our AI scoring orchestra — multiple specialized agents analyze UI quality, code structure, speed, and editability. Scores include AI reasoning for full transparency.
faq.q4: Do I need accounts on each builder?
faq.a4: No. For live v0 runs we handle execution through our integration. Benchmark profiles for other builders do not require your accounts.
faq.a5: We earn through referral partnerships with builders. When you choose to continue with a builder, we may receive a commission. Your results are never biased — scoring is blind and auditable.
auth.registerSuccess: Check your email — click the link to confirm your account.
guest.limitDesc: You've used {limit} free experiments. Sign up to continue without limits!
share.enablePublic: Enable public mode for others to see this experiment.

byoa.title: Bring Your Own API (BYOA)
byoa.subtitle: Connect your own builder accounts (OAuth/API keys). Credentials are safely stored as vault references.
byoa.vaultNotice: Your API key is encrypted and stored in Supabase Vault. It is only used when you dispatch runs for this builder.
byoa.disconnected: API key disconnected. Runs will use the platform key (broker mode) where available.
byoa.disconnectConfirmDesc: Your key will be removed from Vault. Future runs for this builder will use the platform integration unless you connect a new key.
public.notFound: Experiment not found or is private.
